text:
**Upstream feedback to Trellis (mapped to the story each proves):**

| Ask | Why (from the code) | Story |
|---|---|---|
| **1. `tx.preview()`** | F1: pipeline already runs on `working`; swap-only write-back. ~30 lines. Unlocks the "never gamble" half. | 3 |
| **2. Serialize audit **explanations** into the trace** | F2a: `TransactionTrace` carries structural change lists + node-id `audit_log`, but not `input_causes`/`dependency_paths`. Agents get *what*, not the shortest *why*. | 3 |
| **3. Audit retention / historical why** | F3: latest-per-key, ephemeral. A `why_at(revision)` or bounded audit ring would remove the "replay to reconstruct history" tax. | 3 |
| **4. Node-label ergonomics** | Core has `debug_name` (`node.rs:172`) but no first-class stable label/registry export; every consumer re-derives it. | 1 |
| **5. Projection-frame pattern + #121** | F4: one output payload type per graph blocks multi-row projections; document the summarized-intent projection idiom and the keep-bulk-out boundary. | 1 |
| **6. Confirm #114 status in docs** | F2: traces serialize today; positioning doc still calls it a gate. Doc fix. | 3 |
| **7. Oracle can't express host-seam correctness** | §4.6: oracle proves incremental==full, not that the host applied the right effect. A "host-conformance" ledger pattern is a `trellis-testing` gap worth naming. | 1 |
